title: Deinze

infobox.value: Rutger De Reu ([CD&V](/wiki/Christian_Democratic_and_Flemish "Christian Democratic and Flemish"))
[CD&V](/wiki/Christian_Democratic_and_Flemish "Christian Democratic and Flemish"), [Open Deinze](/wiki/Open_Flemish_Liberals_and_Democrats "Open Flemish Liberals and Democrats")
This section **needs expansion**. You can help by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Deinze&action=edit&section=). *(March 2019)*

(Dutch pronunciation: [[ˈdɛinzə]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Nl-Deinze.ogg "File:Nl-Deinze.ogg")) is a [city](/wiki/City_status_in_Belgium "City status in Belgium") and a [municipality](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Belgium "Municipalities of Belgium") in the [Belgian](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium") province of [East Flanders](/wiki/East_Flanders "East Flanders"). It comprises the city of Deinze, and the towns of [Astene](/wiki/Astene "Astene"), Bachte-Maria-Leerne, Gottem, Grammene, [Hansbeke](/wiki/Hansbeke "Hansbeke"), Landegem, Meigem, Merendree, [Nevele](/wiki/Nevele "Nevele"), [Petegem-aan-de-Leie](/wiki/Petegem-aan-de-Leie "Petegem-aan-de-Leie"), [Poesele](/w/index.php?title=Poesele&action=edit&redlink=1 "Poesele (page does not exist)") [[nl](https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Poesele "nl:Poesele")], Sint-Martens-Leerne, Vinkt, [Vosselare](/wiki/Vosselare "Vosselare"), Wontergem, and [Zeveren](/wiki/Zeveren "Zeveren"). On 1 January 2022, Deinze had a population of 44,315. The municipality's total area is 128.03 km2 (49 sq mi), giving a [population density](/wiki/Population_density "Population density") of 342 inhabitants per km2.
Catalogue Spécialisé des Oblitérations Belges, 1849-1910, Nationale en Internationale Postzegelmanifestaties Antwerpen (NIPA), Antwerp, 1999.
Liste des Numéros Postaux, Administration des Postes, Bruxelles 1969.

**Deinze** (Dutch pronunciation: [[ˈdɛinzə]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Nl-Deinze.ogg "File:Nl-Deinze.ogg")) is a [city](/wiki/City_status_in_Belgium "City status in Belgium") and a [municipality](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Belgium "Municipalities of Belgium") in the [Belgian](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium") province of [East Flanders](/wiki/East_Flanders "East Flanders"). It comprises the city of Deinze, and the towns of [Astene](/wiki/Astene "Astene"), Bachte-Maria-Leerne, Gottem, Grammene, [Hansbeke](/wiki/Hansbeke "Hansbeke"), Landegem, Meigem, Merendree, [Nevele](/wiki/Nevele "Nevele"), [Petegem-aan-de-Leie](/wiki/Petegem-aan-de-Leie "Petegem-aan-de-Leie"), [Poesele](/w/index.php?title=Poesele&action=edit&redlink=1 "Poesele (page does not exist)") [[nl](https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Poesele "nl:Poesele")], Sint-Martens-Leerne, Vinkt, [Vosselare](/wiki/Vosselare "Vosselare"), Wontergem, and [Zeveren](/wiki/Zeveren "Zeveren"). On 1 January 2022, Deinze had a population of 44,315. The municipality's total area is 128.03 km2 (49 sq mi), giving a [population density](/wiki/Population_density "Population density") of 342 inhabitants per km2.

On 1 January 2019, the municipality of [Nevele](/wiki/Nevele "Nevele") was merged into Deinze.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deinze&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [[icon]](/wiki/File:Wiki_letter_w_cropped.svg) | This section **needs expansion**. You can help by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Deinze&action=edit&section=). *(March 2019)* |

In 1695, during the [Nine Years' War](/wiki/Nine_Years%27_War "Nine Years' War"), an English force garrisoned in the town under the command of the Irish general [Francis Fergus O’Farrell](/wiki/Francis_Fergus_O%E2%80%99Farrell "Francis Fergus O’Farrell") was forced to surrender to French forces.

### Postal history

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deinze&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Postal history")]

The DEYNZE post office opened in 1836 with the postal code 31 (before 1864), then 94 prior to 1874. The only other office in the area before 1910 was PETEGHEM (not to be confused with PETEGHEM-LEZ-AUDENAERDE), which opened 1 June 1874.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Postal codes in 1969[[3]](#cite_note-3) (before the merger of municipalities in 1977):

* 9800 Deinze
* 9801 Astene
* 9802 [Petegem-aan-de-Leie](/wiki/Petegem-aan-de-Leie "Petegem-aan-de-Leie")
* 9803 Gottem
* 9804 Grammene
* 9805 Wontergem
* 9806 Vinkt
* 9807 [Zeveren](/wiki/Zeveren "Zeveren")
* 9852 Sint-Martens-Leerne
* 9853 Bachte-Maria-Leerne
* 9854 Meigem

## Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deinze&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Sports")]

Deinze is the starting location of the [cycling race](/wiki/Cycling_race "Cycling race") [Gent–Wevelgem](/wiki/Gent%E2%80%93Wevelgem "Gent–Wevelgem"). It is also the operational base of the [Ineos Grenadiers](/wiki/Ineos_Grenadiers "Ineos Grenadiers") [cycle racing](/wiki/Cycle_racing "Cycle racing") team, formally [Team Sky](/wiki/Team_Sky "Team Sky").

## Places of interest

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deinze&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Places of interest")]

* [Museum van Deinze en de Leiestreek](/wiki/Museum_van_Deinze_en_de_Leiestreek "Museum van Deinze en de Leiestreek"), a museum dedicated to arts and folk objects created in the [Leie](/wiki/Leie "Leie") region
* [Ooidonk Castle](/wiki/Ooidonk_Castle "Ooidonk Castle"), a castle dating from the 16th century
* [Filliers](/wiki/Filliers "Filliers") Grain Distillery, a gin distillery

## Famous inhabitants

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deinze&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Famous inhabitants")]

* [Dirk van Braeckel](/wiki/Dirk_van_Braeckel "Dirk van Braeckel"), head of design at Bentley Motors
* [Karel Justinus Calewaert](/wiki/Karel_Justinus_Calewaert "Karel Justinus Calewaert"), Roman Catholic bishop
* [Raoul De Keyser](/wiki/Raoul_De_Keyser "Raoul De Keyser"), painter
* [Rudy Dhaenens](/wiki/Rudy_Dhaenens "Rudy Dhaenens"), cyclist, [World Champion](/wiki/UCI_World_Championships "UCI World Championships") in 1990
* [Poppo of Deinze](/wiki/Poppo_of_Deinze "Poppo of Deinze"), saint
* [Jacques Rogge](/wiki/Jacques_Rogge "Jacques Rogge"), former president of the [International Olympic Committee](/wiki/International_Olympic_Committee "International Olympic Committee")
* [Charles Felix Van Quickenborne](/wiki/Charles_Felix_Van_Quickenborne "Charles Felix Van Quickenborne"), founder of [Saint Louis University](/wiki/Saint_Louis_University_(United_States) "Saint Louis University (United States)") in St. Louis, Mo., USA
* [Karel Van Wijnendaele](/wiki/Karel_Van_Wijnendaele "Karel Van Wijnendaele"), founder of the [Tour of Flanders](/wiki/Tour_of_Flanders_(men%27s_race) "Tour of Flanders (men's race)")
* [Tanneke Sconyncx](/wiki/Tanneke_Sconyncx "Tanneke Sconyncx"), alleged [witch](/wiki/Witch "Witch")
* [Rose Bertram](/wiki/Rose_Bertram "Rose Bertram"), fashion model
